Ziqi Kang Dec 10th, 2024Tumor microenvironment landscape of Chemo-naive HGSC patientsSPACE progress report
1

Project overview
SPACE: Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancer

17-marker panel reveals 10 cell phenotypes across 79 samples, 58561942 cells
TO-DO: Lina and I can try again to phenotype the failed images
Total 90 samplesExperiment details: 
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Wepp0uhCAz9JM2IQpI58oSJxO4wLON5i/edit?gid=620296487#gid=620296487

UMAP projections show low batch eﬀect and well separation among phenotypes

UMAP projections show low batch eﬀect and well separation among phenotypes

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 200 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. 
•Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 12 RCNs
P .S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency

Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in diﬀerent clinical groups
T-cell niche is enriched in progression group.

Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in diﬀerent clinical groupsB-cell, Stromal-CAF and Stroma-residence immune niches are enriched in HRP group.

RCNs lost information of per-sample heterogeneity

UTAG reveals ﬁne-grained domains per-sample, enables further exploration
Use marker intensity as input, 
cell phenotype to interpret

UTAG reveals ﬁne-grained domains with per-sample heterogeneity
Radius: 60px ≈ 20um 
(the value in the paper)
Leiden clustering
Resolution: 0.2
Over-clustered!

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: 
Domain * cell type%
Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: 
Domain * cell type%
Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Tissue domains are continuum http://www.statistik.lmu.de/~eugster/publications/ talk-2012-HIIT-archetypes.pdf

Take home message: •With a 17-marker panel we successfully phenotyped ~60M cells from 85 chemo-naive HGSC patients. 
•Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) catches cross-sample consistency, and show niches enrichment among diﬀerent clinical groups. 
•Calculate tissue domains per sample can help catch per-sample heterogeneity, and can be described as a continuum. 
•For NK and Treg (scattered cell types), we need point pattern analysis, not RCNs or Domains analysis. 
•Current goal is to guide ROIs selection for the Xenium experiment!

Future steps & Remaining questions
Future steps: •Cell phenotyping on problematic images with Lina
•All clinical info will be ready by the end of this week
•UTAG analysis - continue
•Spatial point pattern proﬁling for NK / Treg cells
Questions: •Do we want CD31+ stromal cells? (Endothelial cell) •In the second batch, 3 patients (S333, S341, and S363) were collected more than 1 sample. We need to drop some samples for the Deep-learning-based H&E analysis?

Supplementary

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 200 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. 
•Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 12 RCNs
P .S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

UTAG domains

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: 
Domain * marker intensity
Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Why need point pattern analysis? 
NK and Treg are invisible in RCNs or Domains.
